entities.name: PTT Exploration and Production Public Company Limited

financial.note: Holds ~63.79% stake; PTTEP is the group's primary cash generator and dividend engine, highly sensitive to PTT's consolidated energy strategy.

financial.entity: Bank of New York Mellon / State Street
financial.note: Hold significant minority stakes (~1.7-1.8%); indicates high foreign institutional ownership and sensitivity to global ESG fund flows.

supply_chain.note: Natural gas accounts for ~70-75% of sales volume; EGAT is the ultimate off-taker for the power sector, ensuring a stable revenue floor.
Rising exploration and production costs (OPEX/CAPEX) in the Middle East and Gulf of Thailand projects can squeeze unit margins.

equity_cross_holdings.note: The backbone of Thailand's energy security; production ramp-up to 800 MMcf/d at Erawan is a key 2026 milestone.
Provides seasonal equity income and serves as a primary 'Decarbonize' strategy project.

competitors.note: Direct competition for concession renewals and new E&P block bidding in Southeast Asia.
Rivalry in regional LNG development and deepwater exploration projects in Malaysia and Australia.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The primary determinant of the Average Selling Price (ASP); PTTEP is the purest upstream play on the SET, thriving when Brent exceeds $65/bbl.

macro_commodity_factors.factor: Natural Gas Price (Henry Hub / Pool Gas)
macro_commodity_factors.logic: Gas prices are less volatile than oil but subject to regulatory 'Pool Gas' pricing shifts in Thailand.
Revenue is largely USD-denominated; a weaker THB acts as a significant tailwind for THB-denominated net profit and dividends.
